name: flutter-orchestrate
description: Coordinates multiple specialized assistants to execute complex Flutter development tasks through a phased, dependency-aware workflow

purpose.overview: Decomposes complex tasks into phases, assigns appropriate assistants to each phase, and executes them in the correct order while respecting dependencies. Enables leveraging specialized expertise from multiple assistants (Build Resolver, TDD Guide, Architect) in a coordinated workflow that handles parallel execution, output passing, and failure recovery.
useCases: Implementing complex features that require multiple types of expertise
Coordinating architectural design, implementation, and testing workflows
Executing multi-phase refactoring with verification at each step
Building features that span UI, business logic, testing, and security concerns
Automating end-to-end development workflows with quality gates
Orchestrating parallel work streams with clear dependencies

task.description: Clear description of the complex task to orchestrate (enclose in quotes if it contains spaces)

workflow.description: Workflow generation mode. 'auto' automatically decomposes task and assigns assistants. 'custom' uses a predefined workflow template.

parallel.description: Enable parallel execution of independent phases. 'true' executes independent phases concurrently, 'false' executes all phases sequentially.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the Flutter project to analyze. If not provided, uses the current workspace directory.

architecturalGuidance.yes: Assign Flutter Architect to Architecture Phase

complexMultiPhase.yes: Assign Flutter Plan to Planning Phase

codeImplementation.yes: Assign General Assistant to Implementation Phase

tddWorkflow.yes: Assign Flutter TDD Guide to Testing Phase (parallel with Implementation)

testsAfterImplementation.yes: Assign Flutter TDD Guide to Testing Phase (after Implementation)

buildErrors.yes: Assign Flutter Build Resolver to Build Resolution Phase

qualityVerification.yes: Assign Flutter Verify to Verification Phase

securityReview.yes: Assign Flutter Security to Security Phase

use: Task requires multiple types of expertise (architecture + testing + security)
Task has clear phases with dependencies
Task benefits from parallel execution of independent work
Task requires coordination between multiple assistants
Task needs comprehensive quality gates at each phase
dontUse: Task is simple and can be handled by a single assistant
Task has no clear phases or dependencies
Task requires frequent human intervention between phases

workflowDesign: Keep phases focused: Each phase should have a single, clear responsibility
Minimize dependencies: Reduce dependencies to enable more parallel execution
Define clear outputs: Ensure each phase produces well-defined outputs for dependent phases
Plan for failure: Consider what happens if each phase fails and design recovery strategies
Use checkpoints: Save checkpoints between critical phases for recovery
Validate early: Run validation phases early to catch issues before expensive phases
performanceOptimization: Enable parallel execution: Use --parallel=true for workflows with independent phases
Order phases strategically: Place fast phases before slow phases when possible
Minimize output size: Keep phase outputs focused and avoid passing unnecessary data
Use caching: Cache expensive computations that multiple phases might need
Monitor progress: Track phase execution times to identify bottlenecks

steps: Identify required expertise areas (architecture, testing, security, etc.)
Create phases for each expertise area
Determine phase dependencies: Architecture → Planning → Implementation
Implementation → Testing (parallel with TDD)
Implementation → Build Resolution (if needed)
Testing → Verification
Implementation → Security
Assign assistants to phases based on expertise
Define phase inputs and outputs
Validate workflow for circular dependencies

actions: Immediate Halt: Stop execution of the failed phase
Cascade Skip: Skip all phases that depend on the failed phase
Preserve Outputs: Keep outputs from successfully completed phases
Failure Report: Generate detailed failure report

recoveryStrategies: Retry Phase: Retry the failed phase with the same inputs
Manual Intervention: Provide guidance for manual fixes before retrying
Skip Phase: Skip the failed phase and continue with independent phases
Abort Workflow: Abort the entire workflow and report partial results

examples.name: Feature Implementation with TDD
examples.task: Implement a user authentication feature with email/password login

phases.name: Architecture Design
phases.task: Design authentication architecture with state management and security

phases.name: Implementation Planning
phases.task: Create detailed implementation plan for authentication feature

phases.name: Test Creation (RED)
phases.task: Write failing tests for authentication logic

phases.name: Implementation (GREEN)
phases.task: Implement authentication logic to pass tests

phases.name: Refactoring (REFACTOR)
phases.task: Refactor implementation while keeping tests green

phases.name: Security Review
phases.task: Audit authentication implementation for security vulnerabilities

examples.executionOrder: arch-001 → plan-001 → test-001 → impl-001 → refactor-001 → security-001 → verify-001

examples.name: Parallel Architecture and Testing
examples.task: Refactor state management from Provider to Riverpod

phases.name: Architecture Analysis
phases.task: Analyze current Provider architecture and design Riverpod migration strategy

phases.name: Existing Test Analysis
phases.task: Analyze existing tests and identify tests that need updates

phases.name: Riverpod Migration
phases.task: Migrate Provider code to Riverpod following migration strategy

phases.name: Test Updates
phases.task: Update tests for Riverpod and ensure all tests pass

phases.name: Verification
phases.task: Verify migration with analysis, tests, and build

examples.executionOrder: Phase 1 (parallel): arch-001, test-001 | Phase 2: impl-001 | Phase 3: test-002 | Phase 4: verify-001

examples.name: Build Error Recovery
examples.task: Fix build errors after dependency update

phases.name: Build Error Analysis
phases.task: Analyze build errors and identify root causes

phases.name: Apply Fixes
phases.task: Apply minimal fixes to resolve build errors

phases.name: Test Execution
phases.task: Run tests to ensure fixes don't break functionality

phases.name: Verification
phases.task: Verify build succeeds and all checks pass

steps: Build a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of phase dependencies
Identify phases with no dependencies (entry points)
Execute entry point phases first
As phases complete, mark their dependents as ready
Execute ready phases (in parallel if enabled)
Repeat until all phases complete or a failure occurs

behavior: Independent phases (no shared dependencies) execute concurrently
Dependent phases wait for all dependencies to complete
Resource management ensures system resources are not overwhelmed
Progress tracking monitors all parallel executions
parallelStrategy.example: Time 0: Start arch-001, test-001 (parallel) | Time 1: arch-001 completes | Time 2: test-001 completes, start impl-001 | Time 3: impl-001 completes, start test-002, verify-001 (parallel)

mechanism: Phase outputs are captured and stored
Dependent phases receive outputs as context
Assistants can reference outputs in their task execution
Outputs are included in the final summary report

consumer.task: Implement authentication using the architecture decisions from arch-001

interface.name: string - Human-readable phase name
interface.task: string - Specific task for the assistant
interface.dependsOn: string[] - Array of phase IDs this phase depends on
interface.outputs: string[] - Array of output identifiers this phase produces
interface.parallel: boolean - Whether this phase can run in parallel with others (optional)

phaseTypes.trigger: Task requires architectural decisions or design patterns
Task requires test creation or TDD workflow
dependencies: Implementation phase (or runs in parallel with TDD)

relatedAssistants.description: Provides architectural guidance (assigned to Architecture phases)
Guides test-driven development (assigned to Testing phases)
Resolves build errors (assigned to Build Resolution phases)

relatedCommands.description: Create implementation plans (can be used as a phase in orchestration)
Run verification checks (can be used as a phase in orchestration)
Run security audits (can be used as a phase in orchestration)

features: Uses a directed acyclic graph (DAG) to represent phase dependencies
Implements topological sort for execution order determination
Supports parallel execution using async/await patterns
Validates workflows before execution to catch errors early
Provides real-time progress updates during execution
Generates comprehensive summary reports with aggregated results

stateManagement.phaseStatus: Tracks each phase as pending, running, completed, failed, or skipped
stateManagement.outputs: Stores outputs from completed phases for dependent phases
stateManagement.progress: Tracks overall workflow progress for reporting
stateManagement.errors: Captures errors and failures for debugging and recovery

troubleshooting.symptom: Workflow validation fails with circular dependency error
solution: Identify the cycle in the dependencies
Remove or reorder dependencies to break the cycle
Ensure phases have a clear linear or tree-like dependency structure

troubleshooting.symptom: A phase fails multiple times with the same error
solution: Review the phase failure report for error details
Check if the phase has all required inputs from dependencies
Verify the assistant assigned to the phase is appropriate
Consider manual intervention to fix the underlying issue
Use /flutter-checkpoint to save progress before retrying

troubleshooting.symptom: Workflow takes longer than expected to complete
solution: Review phase dependencies to identify unnecessary sequential execution
Consider breaking large phases into smaller, parallelizable phases
Check if any phase is waiting for external resources (network, file I/O)
Monitor phase execution times to identify bottlenecks

troubleshooting.issue: Output Not Passed to Dependent Phase
troubleshooting.symptom: Dependent phase doesn't receive expected outputs from previous phase
solution: Verify the previous phase completed successfully
Check the output type names match between producer and consumer
Review the phase output structure in the summary report
Ensure the dependent phase explicitly references the required outputs
Check for typos in output type names

usageExamples.command: /flutter-orchestrate "Add a settings screen with theme toggle"

usageExamples.workflow: Architecture → Planning → Security Review → Implementation → Testing → Build Resolution → Final Verification

usageExamples.command: /flutter-orchestrate "Refactor authentication logic to use clean architecture" --parallel=true
usageExamples.workflow: Parallel: Architecture Analysis, Existing Test Analysis | Sequential: Implementation, Test Updates, Refactoring, Verification
Uses a predefined workflow template for user profile features with standard phases

preExecution: Circular Dependency Detection: Ensures no phase depends on itself directly or indirectly
Assistant Availability: Verifies all assigned assistants are available
Phase Completeness: Ensures all phases have required fields (id, name, assistant, task)
Dependency Validity: Verifies all dependsOn references point to valid phase IDs
Output Consistency: Checks that dependent phases can consume outputs from their dependencies

steps: For each phase: Mark phase as 'visiting'
For each dependency: If dependency is 'visiting': CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY DETECTED
If dependency is 'unvisited': Recursively visit dependency
Mark phase as 'visited'
circularDependencyDetection.errorExample:
Error: Circular dependency detected in workflow
Cycle: impl-001 → test-001 → refactor-001 → impl-001

Phase impl-001 depends on refactor-001
Phase refactor-001 depends on test-001
Phase test-001 depends on impl-001

Workflow cannot be executed. Please revise phase dependencies.

onFailure: Error Report: Detailed error message with specific validation failure
Dependency Visualization: Shows the dependency graph with the problematic cycle highlighted
Suggestions: Provides suggestions for fixing the workflow definition
No Execution: Workflow is not executed until validation passes

steps.name: Environment Detection
steps.description: Detects Flutter/FVM setup (checks for .fvm directory or .fvmrc, determines if commands should use 'fvm flutter' or 'flutter')

steps.name: Task Analysis
steps.description: Analyzes the complex task to identify required expertise areas and deliverables

steps.name: Phase Decomposition
steps.description: Breaks down the task into logical phases with clear inputs, outputs, and success criteria

steps.name: Assistant Assignment
steps.description: Assigns appropriate specialized assistants to each phase based on expertise requirements

steps.name: Dependency Resolution
steps.description: Identifies dependencies between phases and builds a directed acyclic graph (DAG)

steps.name: Workflow Validation
steps.description: Validates the workflow for circular dependencies and ensures all phases are executable

steps.name: Phase Execution
steps.description: Executes phases in dependency order, with parallel execution of independent phases

steps.name: Output Passing
steps.description: Passes outputs from completed phases to dependent phases as inputs

steps.name: Progress Tracking
steps.description: Monitors phase execution and provides real-time progress updates

steps.name: Summary Generation
steps.description: Aggregates results from all phases into a comprehensive summary report
